In summary, the terms and conditions of employment for a contractor hired by JDA Software Group, Inc. (the "Recruiter") to provide consulting services to the Provender are set forth in this Agreement ("Agreement"). The Recruiter agrees to provide the services as outlined in this Agreement and is bound by its terms and conditions.

1. Employment. The Recruiter shall be employed by the Recruiter for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement, subject to renewal upon mutual agreement. The Recruiter agrees to provide services as outlined in this Agreement and is bound by its terms and conditions.

2. Compensation. The Recruiter shall be compensated for all services provided under this Agreement. The Recruiter shall be entitled to a lump sum payment upon the expiration of the one-year term of employment, which shall be paid in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in Section 10.

3. Evaluation Material. The Recruiter agrees that it will not solicit or employ any officers or employees of the Provender within one year from the date of this Agreement. If a court of competent jurisdiction, pursuant to Section 9, determines that the Recruiter has breached this Agreement, the Recruiter shall pay the reasonable costs (including legal fees and expenses) incurred by the Provender in enforcing this Agreement.

4. Non-Solicitation. The Recruiter agrees not to solicit or employ any officers or employees of the Provender within one year from the date of this Agreement, except for the purpose of providing consulting services to the Provender.

5. Confidentiality. The Recruiter agrees that it will maintain the confidentiality of all information provided by the Provender in connection with the provision of consulting services.

6. Non-Disclosure. The Recruiter agrees not to disclose any confidential or proprietary information provided by the Provender, including but not limited to trade secrets, financial information, and customer lists.

7. Termination. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party at any time for any reason, with or without cause. In

Explanation:

This Agreement is entered into as of March 1, 2015 between AUSPEX and TEVA Pharmaceutical Industries, Ltd. (“Company”) for the purpose of establishing a global collaboration to develop and commercialize innovative therapies for the treatment of respiratory diseases. The parties have agreed to this Agreement by executing a memorandum of understanding (“MOU”) on February 25, 2015.

The scope of this collaboration includes the following activities:

1. Research and Development: The parties will collaborate in research and development efforts for the discovery, development, and commercialization of new therapies for respiratory diseases. This includes identifying targets, developing drug candidates, conducting preclinical studies, and clinical trials.

2. Commercialization: The parties will work together to develop and commercialize these therapies globally. This includes establishing sales and marketing strategies, negotiating distribution agreements, and managing the supply chain.

3. Intellectual Property: The parties will cooperate in protecting and enforcing their intellectual property rights related to the collaboration. This includes sharing information on patents, trademarks, and copyrights.

4. Financial Obligations: The parties have agreed to a financial obligation for the development of these therapies. This includes funding research and development activities, payments for clinical trials, and milestone payments upon the achievement of certain milestones.

5. Intellectual Property: The parties will cooperate in protecting and enforcing their intellectual property rights related to the collaboration. This includes sharing information on patents, trademarks, and copyrights.

6. Confidentiality: Both parties agree not to disclose any confidential information or proprietary data related to the collaboration. This includes non-public information about the collaborating parties, their products, and their businesses.

7. Termination: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice if a material breach occurs. In such cases, the other party will have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately.

8. Headquarters: The parties agree that the headquarters for this collaboration will be located in La Jolla, California

The Confirmation and Agreement for the Transaction between Orchestra-Prémaman, S.A. And Destination Maternity Corporation (“Company”) is hereby agreed upon by and between the parties hereto as follows:

1. Introduction:

Orchestra-Prémaman, S.A. Is a French company that specializes in providing music education services to students of all ages. The Company is a Delaware corporation engaged in the business of providing online educational resources for students and teachers.

2. Purpose of the Transaction:

The purpose of this Agreement is to establish a definitive agreement between the Company and Orchestra-Prémaman, S.A., whereby the Company will acquire all of the outstanding shares of common stock of the Company for an amount equal to $100,000, plus a one-time cash payment of $50,000.

3. Terms and Conditions:

The following terms and conditions shall apply to this Agreement:

a) The Company will pay the purchase price to the Company’s Board of Directors (“Board”) in cash within 14 days of the closing date.

b) The Company will issue a press release announcing the closing of the transaction and providing details about the terms and conditions of the transaction, including the name of the acquiring company, the name of the acquiring shareholder, the number of shares acquired by the acquiring company, and the purchase price paid.

c) The Company will provide a copy of this press release to the Board within 14 days of the closing date.

d) The Company will pay the one-time cash payment to the Company’s Board within 30 days of the closing date.

e) The Company will continue to operate as an independent entity under its existing name and logo, and will not dissolve or cease operations during the Transaction period.

f) The Company will provide a copy of this Agreement to the Board within 14 days of the closing date.

g) The Company will maintain all records and documents related to the transaction for a period of at least three years after the closing date.

h) The Company will not solicit or engage in any discussions with any third parties regarding the Transaction during the Transaction period

The following is a summary of the key provisions in the Agreement for the Continuous Service Bonus and Employee Retention Plan between the First Bank of Beverly Hills, California (the "Bank") and Joseph W. Kiley, III ("Employee"). The Agreement was signed on December 21, 2005, and is effective as of January 1, 2006.

The Continuous Service Bonus Plan provides for a one-time bonus payment to Employee in the amount of $75,000.00 if Employee remains employed through June 30, 2006. If the Bank terminates Employee's employment prior to June 30, 2006, Employee will remain eligible to receive this continuous service bonus payment.

The Employee Retention Plan provides for a flow-through of all amounts and benefits due hereunder to Employee and if such amounts and benefits are not timeously paid or provided by the Bank, such amounts and benefits shall be paid or provided by the Company. The Company guarantees payment and provision of all amounts and benefits due from the Bank.

The Agreement incorporates by reference all applicable regulatory law, including California law. The notice requirements for any notice required under this Agreement are set forth in Section 8.10.

The parties acknowledge that they have had an opportunity to negotiate, carefully consider, and receive the advice of their respective attorneys before signing this Agreement. To the extent that either party does not seek the advice of an attorney, it knowsingly and freely waives such right.

This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the Bank, its successor(s) and assigns, and upon the Employee and his heirs, executors, administrators and legal representatives. No party to this Agreement may delegate its or his duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the other parties to this Agreement.

The Agreement shall be governed by California law, and any disputes arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved in accordance with the laws of the State of California.
